title: WHAT / SOMETHING
objective: Memorize and replay escalating pulse sequences on the WHAT and SOMETHING pads, preserving your combo while stripping out sudden overbuilt fake beats.

controls.input: Tap the glowing WHAT or SOMETHING pad
controls.outcome: Plays that beat back in sequence and scores if correct

controls.input: Tap and hold a pad on a beat wrapped in extra rings, then release on the flash
controls.outcome: Converts an overbuilt pulse into its clean version for bonus points

controls.input: Hit the center meter after a full correct sequence
controls.outcome: Locks in your current multiplier before the next faster round starts

steps: A round begins with 3-5 pulses alternating between the WHAT and SOMETHING pads, each with distinct tones.
The sequence replays once at full speed, then the player must repeat it from memory.
Every few seconds, one beat becomes an overbuilt pulse: it looks louder, adds visual clutter, and must be held then released instead of just
correct replays raise the combo multiplier and add one new pulse to the next round.
After each successful round, the player chooses speed implicitly by banking now for safety or letting the streak roll for a bigger next-se
One wrong input, a missed hold-release, or failing to bank before the overload meter fills ends the run instantly.
scoring: Score comes from correct beats, perfect timing, and simplified overbuilt pulses. Consecutive perfect rounds raise a 1x to 8x multiplier. Banked streaks post to a simple offline local leaderboard of highest single-run lab
tips: Use the tone as much as the color; WHAT is lower and SOMETHING is higher.
Overbuilt pulses are worth more, but they also speed up the overload meter if you hesitate.
Bank early in the last minute if you want a leaderboard-safe run; greedier streaks win bigger scores.

trendContext.title: What’s something new game devs over-engineer that experienced teams keep simple?
